channel.title: Global Sanctions News (Custom RSS)
channel.description: Auto-generated feed from rendered Global Sanctions news list

item.title: UK adds 4 people and 7 entities to Sudan Sanctions list for participating in illicit gold and finance networks
item.description: The UK has added 4 people and 7 entities to the Sudan sanctions list under the Sudan (Sanctions) (EU Exit) Regulations 2020 – press release. The designations are said to target the conflict gold trade

item.title: EU General Court rejects claims brought by Lithuanian company against prohibitions on the transfer of potash products from Belarus through the EU
item.description: T‑261/24, Birių Krovinių Terminalas v Council – The General Court of the EU has rejected a series of claims brought by Lithuanian company Birių Krovinių Terminalas (“BKT”). BKT (a transit operator tha

item.title: EU statement on 3rd country alignment with Reg 269/2014 designations
item.description: The EU has issued a statement saying that Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Iceland, Liechtenstein, Moldova, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Norway and Ukraine have aligned with the EU’s decision to add 9

item.title: US-Iranian national convicted of breaching US sanctions on Iran
item.description: A federal jury in Boston has convicted Mahdi Mohammad Sadeghi, a dual US-Iranian national, of illegally exporting sophisticated electronic components to Iran. Mr Sadeghi worked at a US microelectro

item.title: DC Circuit rejects appeal on OFAC Russia designation of precious metal traders under EO 14024
item.description: Diegelmann v. Bessent, No. 24-5277 (D.C. Cir. 2026): The Court of Appeals for the DC Circuit dismissed an appeal against a District Court judgment which held that OFAC acted lawfully in designating Ax

item.title: US makes Iran designations linked to Shamkhani shipping network
item.description: The US has added over 50 people, entities and vessels linked to Mohammad Hossein Shamkhani and his shipping network to its EO 13902 Iran sanctions list – press release. Mr Shamkhani was US-sanction

item.title: Deutsche Bank and UniCredit bring sanctions loss claims against Linde
item.description: The FT has reported that Deutsche Bank and UniCredit have brought claims against German gas company Linde to recover assets seized by courts in Russia. In 2021, a consortium including Linde Enginee

item.title: UN Libya committee issues guidance on reinvesting frozen LIA reserves
item.description: The UN Security Council Committee concerning Libya has published Implementation Assistance Notice 8, which explains how Member States and financial institutions should apply paragraph 14 of Security C

item.title: US considers further ICC sanctions
item.description: The US has announced a new policy towards the International Criminal Court. Secretary of State Marco Rubio wrote in the Wall Street Journal that the US is launching a diplomatic campaign to “dismantle

item.title: EU adds 15 people and 1 entity to human rights sanctions list
item.description: The EU has designated 15 people and 1 entity under its Regulation (EU) 2020/1998 human rights sanctions list – press release. See our EU human rights pages for information on the Reg 2020/1998 list, i
